Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows markedly elevated serum creatinine, proteinuria, glucosuria, and ketonuria, suggesting significant renal dysfunction, likely acute or chronic kidney disease with possible diabetic ketoacidosis. Low serum CO₂ may indicate metabolic acidosis. Urine is cloudy, brown, and has low specific gravity, further supporting renal impairment. Immediate clinical evaluation and nephrology consultation are recommended.
